https://github.com/godotengine/godot/com.commit_message:
Merge pull request #114234 from passivestar/inspector-category-spacing

Improve editor inspector main container spacing in modern theme

ranked_scenarios.summary: Fix issues with property height in the inspector

description.text: - Fix separation between properties not updating on style change. - Reworked property sizing code. - Fix property sizes not being uniform on the Modern theme: Fixes #112393. Fixes #113542.

closed_issues.summary: Inconsistent property editor sizing in new editor theme

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in: v4.6-dev3 with the modern theme enabled - Not reproducible in: any version before v4.6-dev3 ### System information Godot v4.6.dev3 - macOS Sequoia (15.6.1) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M2 Pro (Apple8) - Apple M2 Pro (12 threads) - 16.00 GiB memory ### Issue description The property editors have inconsistent padding in the new theme. Here's an example in the `Editor Settings` menu: Checkbox property editors for options like `Expand to Title` and `Use Native File Dialogs` have more padding then the integer property editors for font sizes. This can also be seen many places in the inspector: Color, float and Vector2 property editors also appear to have much smaller padding than the boolean and enum property editors. I'm not sure if this is an intentional design decision or not, but I personally found the uneven sizing of these elements to be a usability issue when trying out the minimal theme before it was adopted as default, and the new recreated version seems to suffer the same problem. The differences in the visual weight for each element are very distracting when trying to find a specific property. That being said, if this isn't the right place for design feedback like this, let me know if there's somewhere else I should submit it and feel free to close this issue :) ### Steps to reproduce Compile and launch the editor. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A
